name: submap_shrinks_pte_size
code:
proof fn phi_1_submap_shrinks_pte_size(pt: Map<nat, PTE>, sub_pt: Map<nat, PTE>, vaddr: nat, ult_id: nat)
    requires
        sub_pt.submap_of(pt),
        pt.contains_key(vaddr),
        !sub_pt.contains_key(vaddr),
    ensures
        os::CoreState::UnmapWaiting { ult_id, vaddr }.pte_size(sub_pt) == 0,
        os::CoreState::UnmapWaiting { ult_id, vaddr }.pte_size(pt) == pt[vaddr].frame.size,
{
}
reason: When a key is in pt but not sub_pt, UnmapWaiting's pte_size drops to 0 — the lemma exploits this monotonicity (smaller regions overlap less), but the zero-size region means the core's operation becomes invisible to overlap checks on the submap

name: overlap_monotone_in_size
code:
proof fn phi_5_overlap_monotone_in_size(base1: nat, size1_small: nat, size1_large: nat, base2: nat, size2_small: nat, size2_large: nat)
    requires
        size1_small <= size1_large,
        size2_small <= size2_large,
        overlap(MemRegion { base: base1, size: size1_small }, MemRegion { base: base2, size: size2_small }),
    ensures
        overlap(MemRegion { base: base1, size: size1_large }, MemRegion { base: base2, size: size2_large }),
{
}
reason: The lemma's proof implicitly relies on overlap being monotone in region sizes — if overlap weren't monotone, the submap-to-supermap reasoning would be unsound
